statement_html:
Soit \(\mathrm{M}\) le point d'abscisse \(x\) sur la droite graduée d'origine \(\mathrm{O}\).
\draw{800,64}{xrange -13,13 yrange -2,2 hline 0,0,red arrow 12,0,13,0,15,red parallel -12,-0.2,-12,0.2,1,0,2,blue parallel -10,-0.4,-10,0.4,5,0,5,blue parallel -9,-0.2,-9,0.2,1,0,4,blue parallel -4,-0.2,-4,0.2,1,0,4,blue parallel 1,-0.2,1,0.2,1,0,4,blue parallel 6,-0.2,6,0.2,1,0,4,blue parallel 11,-0.2,11,0.2,1,0,2,blue text red,-0.1,1,medium,O text red,2* random(1,-1)-0.1,1,medium,B text red,1*random(1,-1)-0.1,1,medium,C text red,-0.1,-0.75,medium,0 text red,2* random(1,-1)-0.1,-0.75,medium,2* random(1,-1) text red,1*random(1,-1)-0.1,-0.75,medium,1*random(1,-1) segment 2* random(1,-1),-0.2,2* random(1,-1),0.2,red segment 1*random(1,-1),-0.2,1*random(1,-1),0.2,red}
Donner l'expression de la distance de \(\mathrm{M}\) à \(\mathrm{B}\) puis de \(\mathrm{C}\) à \(\mathrm{M}\), à l'aide d'une valeur absolue.
\special{mathmlinput [\mathrm{M B}=\left|reply1\right|],5,noanswer reply 1 }
\special{mathmlinput [\mathrm{C M}=\left|reply2\right|],5,noanswer reply 2 }
